commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=8c226aec8605dfaec4a7e719184befabfefb702d)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes recurring cron error scheduling to use retry hints and configured backoff before the next natural schedule slot, with regression tests for daily cron retries, unresolved next-runs, backoff floors, and exhausted retry budgets.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main can choose the next daily natural cron slot over a short retry backoff, and the PR adds a focused regression for that exact recurring error path.
commands.review_followup: The remaining blockers are maintainer/proof handling and a public config-contract decision, not a safe automated repair lane because contributor real-environment proof cannot be generated for them.

target.title: docs(memory): align descriptors and docs with recursive memory/**/*.md
target.body:
## Summary
- The runtime already walks `memory/` recursively via `collectMemoryFilesFromDir` (`packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/internal.ts`), so nested files like `memory/YYYY-MM/YYYY-MM-DD.md` are indexed and searched. But several agent-facing descriptors and public docs still said `memory/*.md`, which can mislead agents into thinking the index is flat.
- This PR updates the remaining stale references to `memory/**/*.md`. No runtime behavior change.

## Files touched
- `extensions/memory-core/index.ts` — lazy `memory_search` / `memory_get` descriptors
- `extensions/memory-core/src/tools.ts` — concrete `memory_search` / `memory_get` descriptions
- `extensions/memory-core/src/prompt-section.ts` — system-prompt recall guidance
- `src/config/schema.help.ts` — `agents.defaults.memorySearch` help text
- `docs/concepts/memory-builtin.md`, `memory.md`, `system-prompt.md`
- `docs/gateway/config-agents.md`, `docs/reference/token-use.md`
- `docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256` regenerated via `pnpm config:docs:gen`

## Note
This supersedes #72748. That PR also carried QMD watcher changes that were independently resolved upstream by #81802 (`watch-settle.ts`), so the surviving value was just this wording alignment. Splitting it out as a small, behavior-neutral PR for easier review.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: Agent-facing descriptors and public docs said `memory/*.md` even though the runtime already walks `memory/` recursively and indexes nested files such as `memory/YYYY-MM/YYYY-MM-DD.md`. This PR aligns the wording to `memory/**/*.md` so agents and readers see what is actually indexed. The change is wording-only; the proof below shows the installed runtime already exhibits the recursive behavior the new wording describes, so the PR just stops misleading the agent.

**Real environment tested**: Installed OpenClaw `2026.5.20` (npm global, current `latest` published build, ahead of the `acf265d` base this PR branches from), against the live workspace `~/.openclaw/workspace-moku` used by the gateway. Agent `main`, local embedding provider, per-agent SQLite memory store at `~/.openclaw/memory/main.sqlite`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

Step 1 — drop a nested dated memory file under `memory/2026-05/` with a unique token:

```
cat > ~/.openclaw/workspace-moku/memory/2026-05/2026-05-24-recursive-proof.md <<'EOF'
Recursive memory search proof.
Unique token for PR 85899 smoke test: PROOFTOKEN_QXZ7K_NESTED_RECURSIVE_MEMORY
This file lives at memory/2026-05/2026-05-24-recursive-proof.md
EOF
```

Step 2 — reindex the live agent's memory store:

```
openclaw memory index
```

Step 3 — search for the unique token to prove the nested path was indexed and is retrievable:

```
openclaw memory search "PROOFTOKEN_QXZ7K_NESTED_RECURSIVE_MEMORY" --max-results 3
```

**After-fix evidence**: Terminal output from the installed `openclaw 2026.5.20` runtime on the live workspace (absolute paths redacted to `~`):

```
$ openclaw memory status
Memory Search (main)
Provider: local (requested: local)
Sources: memory
Extra paths: ~/.openclaw/shared-memory
Indexed: 105/105 files . 933 chunks
Workspace: ~/.openclaw/workspace-moku
By source:
  memory . 105/105 files . 933 chunks
```

```
$ openclaw memory index
[node-llama-cpp] ... falling back to using no GPU
Memory index updated (main).
```

```
$ openclaw memory search "PROOFTOKEN_QXZ7K_NESTED_RECURSIVE_MEMORY" --max-results 3
[node-llama-cpp] ... falling back to using no GPU
0.812 memory/2026-05/2026-05-24-recursive-proof.md:1-9
    Recursive memory search proof.
    Unique token for PR 85899 smoke test: PROOFTOKEN_QXZ7K_NESTED_RECURSIVE_MEMORY
    This file lives at memory/2026-05/2026-05-24-recursive-proof.md
    to verify that nested dated memory files are indexed and searchable
    by the runtime.

0.450 memory/fix-openclaw/memory-recursive-search/smoke/2026-04-27.md:1-6
0.432 memory/fix-openclaw/memory-recursive-search/pr-draft.md:1-32
```

**Observed result after fix**: The freshly written nested file `memory/2026-05/2026-05-24-recursive-proof.md` (two levels deep under `memory/`) was indexed by `openclaw memory index` and returned as the top match (score `0.812`) for the unique token. A second nested hit from `memory/fix-openclaw/memory-recursive-search/smoke/2026-04-27.md` (three levels deep) confirms recursion is not limited to one level. This is the actual indexing behavior already shipped in the installed runtime; the PR diff is purely descriptor and doc string changes plus a regenerated `docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256`, with no runtime code paths modified.

**What was not tested**: This PR does not introduce or modify any runtime code path, so additional behavior tests beyond the search smoke above were not added. The recursive walk itself (`collectMemoryFilesFromDir`) is already covered by upstream tests under `packages/memory-host-sdk/`.

## Test plan
- [x] `pnpm config:docs:gen` regenerates baseline cleanly (no other drift)
- [x] Nested memory smoke search on installed `2026.5.20` returns the new nested file at score `0.812`
- [ ] CI: lint / type / config-baseline check

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: yetval

target.title: fix(cron): retry transient errors before next natural cron slot (#85888)
target.body:
## Summary

- Recurring cron error path used `Math.max(naturalNext, backoffNext)` for the next scheduled run. For daily schedules (e.g. `0 5 * * *`), `naturalNext` is ~24h ahead and always dominated the short backoff, so retries for transient provider errors were effectively suppressed.
- This matches the symptom in #85888: scheduled jobs hitting MiniMax `overloaded_error` (503) during 05:00-07:30 Asia/Shanghai never re-fired the same day, while manual reruns at 08:30+ (after the busy window) succeeded because they bypassed the broken scheduler path.
- While retry budget is available and the error is classified retryable via `resolveCronExecutionRetryHint`, schedule `min(naturalNext, backoffNext)`. Once `consecutiveErrors > retryConfig.maxAttempts` or the error is permanent, fall back to `naturalNext` through the existing `resolveCronNextRunWithLowerBound` guard. When croner cannot resolve the natural next slot for a cron schedule, the retry path also clears `nextRunAtMs` to preserve the unresolved-next-run guard from #66019.

Closes #85888

## Files

- `src/cron/service/timer.ts` — split recurring error path into "within retry budget" (use `min(naturalNext, backoffNext)`, preserve unresolved-next-run guard) vs. "exhausted / permanent" (use natural slot via existing lower-bound resolver).
- `src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts` — three regression tests: (1) backoff retry within same day on retryable daily-cron error, (2) clears schedule when next-run is unresolved during retry, (3) fallback to natural slot when retry budget exhausted.

## Real behavior proof

I am an external contributor and do not have a live OpenClaw + MiniMax setup to capture redacted runtime cron logs. The regression tests in `timer.regression.test.ts` exercise the exact `applyJobResult` failure path with the same `FailoverError: ... overloaded_error ... status=503` error text reported in the issue against a daily `0 5 * * *` schedule in `Asia/Shanghai`, and assert the next-run timestamp now lands within the same day instead of being pushed to the next natural slot. Source-level reasoning for the original misbehavior is documented in #85888's clawsweeper comment (timer.ts:1008 used `Math.max`, which always picked the ~24h-away naturalNext over the few-minute backoff for daily crons).

Requesting maintainer review for a `proof:` override or guidance on capturing acceptable runtime evidence.

## Test plan

- [ ] `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts`
- [ ] `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/retry-hint.test.ts`
- [ ] `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service.jobs.test.ts`
- [ ] CI green on cron suite

## Review responses

- **ClawSweeper P1 (`timer.ts:1016-1019` unresolved-next-run guard):** Addressed in the second commit. When retry budget is available but croner returns `undefined` for `naturalNext` on a cron schedule, the retry path now clears `nextRunAtMs` and logs the same `cron: next run unresolved; clearing schedule to avoid a refire loop` warning instead of firing on bare backoff. Regression added.

- **ClawSweeper P1 (`timer.ts:1028` backoff floor):** Addressed in the third commit. The retry-budget branch now uses `backoffNext` directly instead of `min(naturalNext, backoffNext)`, so high-frequency recurring schedules (e.g. every-1s) cannot bypass the configured backoff via a sooner natural slot. Daily-cron retry path from #85888 still works because `backoffNext` lands within the same day while `naturalNext` is ~24h away. New regression covers an every-1s job: `nextRunAtMs >= endedAt + everyMs`, proving the backoff floor is preserved.
target.author: yetval
